2604.12231.title: Thought-Retriever: Don't Just Retrieve Raw Data, Retrieve Thoughts for Memory-Augmented Agentic Systems
2604.12231.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have transformed AI research thanks to their powerful internal capabilities and knowledge. However, existing LLMs still fail to effectively incorporate the massive external knowledge when interacting with the world. Although retrieval-augmented LLMs are proposed to mitigate the issue, they are still fundamentally constrained by the context length of LLMs, as they can only retrieve top-K raw data chunks from the external knowledge base which often consists of millions of data chunks. Here we propose Thought-Retriever, a novel model-agnostic algorithm that helps LLMs generate output conditioned on arbitrarily long external data, without being constrained by the context length or number of retrieved data chunks. Our key insight is to let an LLM fully leverage its intermediate responses generated when solving past user queries (thoughts), filtering meaningless and redundant thoughts, organizing them in thought memory, and retrieving the relevant thoughts when addressing new queries. This effectively equips LLM-based agents with a self-evolving long-term memory that grows more capable through continuous interaction. Besides algorithmic innovation, we further meticulously prepare a novel benchmark, AcademicEval, which requires an LLM to faithfully leverage ultra-long context to answer queries based on real-world academic papers. Extensive experiments on AcademicEval and two other public datasets validate that Thought-Retriever remarkably outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving an average increase of at least 7.6% in F1 score and 16% in win rate across various tasks. More importantly, we further demonstrate two exciting findings: (1) Thought-Retriever can indeed help LLM self-evolve after solving more user queries; (2) Thought-Retriever learns to leverage deeper thoughts to answer more abstract user queries.
2604.12231.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）因其強大的內部能力和知識而改變了人工智慧研究。<lb/>然而，現有的LLMs在與世界互動時仍然無法有效地整合大量的外部知識。<lb/>儘管提出了增強檢索的LLMs以緩解這一問題，但它們仍然在根本上受到LLMs的上下文長度的限制，因為它們只能從外部知識庫中檢索前K個原始數據塊，而這些數據庫通常由數百萬個數據塊組成。<lb/>在此，我們提出了Thought-Retriever，一種新穎的模型無關算法，幫助LLMs生成基於任意長外部數據的輸出，而不受上下文長度或檢索數據塊數量的限制。<lb/>我們的關鍵見解是讓LLM充分利用其在解決過去用戶查詢時生成的中間響應（思考），過濾無意義和冗餘的思考，將其組織在思考記憶中，並在處理新查詢時檢索相關的思考。<lb/>這有效地為基於LLM的代理提供了一種自我演化的長期記憶，通過持續互動而變得更加強大。<lb/>除了算法創新，我們還仔細準備了一個新基準，AcademicEval，該基準要求LLM忠實地利用超長上下文來回答基於現實世界學術論文的查詢。<lb/>在AcademicEval和另外兩個公共數據集上的廣泛實驗驗證了Thought-Retriever顯著超越了最先進的基準，實現了F1分數平均提高至少7.6%和各種任務的勝率提高16%。<lb/>更重要的是，我們進一步展示了兩個令人興奮的發現：（1）Thought-Retriever確實可以幫助LLM在解決更多用戶查詢後自我演化；（2）Thought-Retriever學會利用更深層的思考來回答更抽象的用戶查詢。
2604.12231.author: Tao Feng et.al.
2604.12231.authors: Tao Feng, Pengrui Han, Guanyu Lin, Ge Liu, Jiaxuan You
